title: senecio toromarensis
intro: Senecio toromarensis, a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, is a rare and endangered succulent native to New Zealand. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. These alkaloids are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Senecio toromarensis can be used as a tincture, tea, or topical cream, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
